checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2015 income statement (implied DSO ~93.3 days, up from ~60.6 days a year earlier)
10-Q Q3 2016 income statement (DSO ~88.6 days, still elevated vs ~60 days baseline)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 2016 balance sheet (total assets +30% vs goodwill/intangibles +>600% in one quarter)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2015 cash flow statement (OCF ~64% of NI, a ~$24.4M gap)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 2016 balance sheet (inventory up ~141% in one quarter)
10-Q Q1 2016 income statement (implied days-inventory jumped from ~136 to ~203 days quarter-over-quarter)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 2016 income statement (sharp reversal, indicating earnings are volatile, not smooth, though the size/timing of the Q4 spike is itself unusual)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: 10-Q Q3 2016 balance sheet, comparative FY2015 (accrued liabilities grew roughly in proportion to revenue, ~2.4-3.7% of revenue across periods)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filing_date=2008-07-29; 10-K/A filing_date=2008-09-30; 10-K/A filing_date=2009-07-29; 10-K/A filing_date=2011-05-02; 10-K/A filing_date=2012-04-27; 10-K/A filing_date=2012-06-21; 10-K/A filing_date=2014-04-30
evidence.location: Filing chronology (seven 10-K/A amendments across the company's history, indicating repeated restatement/correction activity)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data spanning 2009-2016 across Assets, Liabilities, Revenue, AR, Inventory, Goodwill, Cash Flow items

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential aggressive/premature revenue recognition or extended customer credit terms, evidenced by rising days-sales-outstanding (DSO) from ~60.6 days (Q4 2014) to ~93.3 days (Q4 2015) and remaining elevated (~87-90 days) through Q3 2016, even as revenue continued to grow rapidly. This could indicate channel stuffing, bill-and-hold arrangements, or insufficient allowance for doubtful accounts inflating both revenue and receivables.

Net income growing faster than, and diverging materially from, operating cash flow in FY2015 (NI $67.4M vs OCF $43.1M, a ~36% shortfall), consistent with earnings driven by non-cash accruals (e.g., receivables build-up) rather than cash-generative operations, raising earnings-quality concerns about the sustainability/accuracy of reported profitability.

An unusually large gap between Q4 2015 net income ($30.6M) and the implied Q4 2015 operating income (~$17.7M, derived from FY2015 operating income of $55.8M less 9-month operating income of $38.0M) suggests a large non-operating item (e.g., tax benefit, valuation allowance release, or other one-time gain) boosted net income in that quarter. Net income then fell sharply to $2.7M in Q1 2016, a pattern consistent with income smoothing or opportunistic timing of non-recurring items, and is corroborated by the company's history of seven 10-K/A amendments, indicating a track record of financial statement corrections.

rationale_evidence.quote: OperatingIncomeLoss=55,786,144.19 (FY2015) vs sum of Q1-Q3 2015 OperatingIncomeLoss=38,038,713.41 (implied Q4=17,747,430.78)

top_signals: Rising DSO from ~60 to ~93 days in FY2015, remaining elevated through Q3 2016, indicating receivables growing faster than revenue
Material divergence between FY2015 net income ($67.4M) and operating cash flow ($43.1M)
Unexplained large Q4 2015 net income spike (~$30.6M) disconnected from implied operating income (~$17.7M), followed by a sharp reversal in Q1 2016
History of seven 10-K/A amendments and one NT 10-Q, indicating recurring financial reporting corrections
Goodwill, intangibles, and inventory each spiking 140-600%+ in Q1 2016, far outpacing ~30% asset/revenue growth, tied to an acquisition with purchase-accounting risk
